prompt: isometric pixel-art game tiles for a top-down RPG map. Each tile is a DIFFERENT small object standing FREE in empty space, resting on a small flat patch of clear blue water; the object rises straight up and IS the sprite. CRITICAL: the entire background is FULLY TRANSPARENT and there is NO black outline, NO dark border, NO frame, NO box, NO rectangle, NO square, NO cube edges and NO wireframe anywhere around the tile or the object — the object's silhouette is soft and organic against empty transparency, with no containing lines or hard straight edges enclosing it. Do NOT stand it on a raised block, cube, column, box, crate or pedestal, and do NOT draw the ground as a raised cube: just a small low patch at its base. Go for maximum variety — different shapes, species, ages and conditions, no two tiles alike. The set: 1) a clump of marsh grass 2) a cluster of green reeds 3) a floating patch of lotus 4) a wooden mooring post 5) a heron standing in the shallows 6) a partly sunken barrel 7) a clump of cattails 8) a floating bottle caught in a reed 9) a small sandbar with a shell 10) a patch of water lilies. cozy hand-crafted top-down RPG pixel art, rich natural texture, cohesive harmonious palette, soft directional shading, high quality game asset, transparent background
